name: SPE 3: Sandstone Reservoir for CO2-EOR
overview: This dataset represents a typical sandstone reservoir suitable for CO2 Enhanced Oil Recovery. It is based on the 3rd SPE Comparative Solution Project (SPE 3) and is widely used as a benchmark for CO2 sequestration and EOR studies. The properties are characteristic of a fluvial-deltaic depositional environment.
reference: Kenyon, D. E., & Behie, A. (1987). Third SPE Comparative Solution Project: Gas Cycling of Retrograde Condensate Reservoirs. Journal of Petroleum Technology, 39(08), 981-997. doi:10.2118/12278-PA

name: Weyburn Field: Carbonate Reservoir (CO2-EOR)
overview: This dataset is representative of the Weyburn Field in Saskatchewan, Canada, a large carbonate reservoir and one of the world's largest CO2-EOR projects. The data reflects a Mississippian-aged carbonate formation with moderate porosity and low permeability, making it a challenging but successful EOR target.
reference: Whittaker, S., et al. (2004). The IEA Weyburn CO2 Monitoring and Storage Project. Paper presented at the 7th International Conference on Greenhouse Gas Control Technologies, Vancouver, BC, Canada. September 2004.

name: Simplified Hypothetical Reservoir
overview: Simplified reservoir simulation dataset for a small, hypothetical reservoir.
